{{Quote|Your friend is chasing air. Soon you'll be too busy trying to get him out of jail. And I will rule New Apsolon.|Alani, to Obi-Wan Kenobi, about Qui-Gon Jinn|<ref name="Jedi Apprentice: The Call to Vengeance">''[[Jedi Apprentice: The Call to Vengeance]]''</ref>}}
'''Alani''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] who was the daughter of [[Ewane]] and twin sister of [[Eritha]].

==Biography==
Alani was born in [[57 BBY/Legends|57 BBY]] on [[New Apsolon/Legends|New Apsolon]]. At this time a totalitarian system of government ruled the planet. [[Civilized|The Civilized]] received all the benefits, while [[Workers|the Workers]] lived in poverty. Alani's father, Ewane, was a leader of the resistance against this social system. Due to this activity he spent many years in the prison and saw his daughters rarely. Alani and Eritha were raised mostly by friends of their father, since their mother had died around the time they were born. One of those friends was [[Lenz]].

With the first elections after the fall of the regime in the year [[47 BBY/Legends|47 BBY]], their father was selected to be [[Supreme Governor]] of New Apsolon. At that time, the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] were sent to supervise the election, and they were represented by [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] and [[Tahl]], who developed a bond with Alani and her twin sister [[Eritha]].

Ewane held the office for five years, but was murdered shortly into his second term. [[Roan]], a close friend of their father and his successor, adopted them. At that time, Alani and Eritha conceived a plan to gain the power on New Apsolon. That plan included killing Roan and kidnapping Tahl, who died as a result of the injuries suffered. However, the plan failed with no small part due to the actions of Qui-Gon Jinn and [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] who came to New Apsolon looking for the missing Tahl. Because Alani wasn't as clever as Eritha, she had bragged to Obi-Wan that Qui-Gon would die in a trap elaborated by her sister, leading Obi-Wan to rescue Qui-Gon. After the failed plan, both Alani and Eritha, along with their associate [[Balog (Human)|Balog]] were arrested.
